channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: I.R.S. to Drop Audits of Trump and Family
item.description: As part of the Justice Department’s deal, officials vowed not to pursue any matters, including those involving President Trump’s tax returns, that are pending.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer, Andrew Duehren and Glenn Thrush
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The document was signed by acting Attorney General Todd Blanche.

item.title: Prison to Pardons to Payouts: Jan. 6 Rioters Are Elated at Trump’s $1.8 Billion Fund
item.description: The possibility that people who ransacked the Capitol could get money from the government they attacked is the latest head-spinning twist in President Trump’s effort to rewrite the history of Jan. 6.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston for The New York Times
item.media:description: The pro-Trump mob at the Capitol in 2021. If all of the Jan. 6ers sought money from the fund and received the same amount, the payouts would be around $1.125 million each.

item.title: With Trump’s Settlement, a Possible $100 Million I.R.S. Penalty Melts Away
item.description: The tax service argued that the Trump Organization tried to claim the same losses twice. The president said the audit was a “disgrace.”
item.dc:creator: Russ Buettner
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: The audit dated back to a $72.9 million tax refund that Donald J. Trump claimed, and received, starting in about 2010.

item.title: New Immunity for President Trump, and an Audacious Plan for Regime Change in Iran
item.description: Plus, Tesla’s big bet on big rigs.
item.dc:creator: Will Jarvis, Margaret Kadifa, Jake Lucas, Ian Stewart, Mark Mazzetti and Reid J. Epstein
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Chang W. Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, the former Iranian president, at the United Nations General Assembly in New York in 2012. An Israeli strike in Tehran was part of an effort to put him in power, U.S. officials said.

item.title: Early War Goal Was to Install Hard Line Former President as Iran’s Leader
item.description: An Israeli strike designed to free Mahmoud Ahmadinejad from house arrest in Tehran, U.S. officials said, was part of an effort to bring about regime change and put him in power.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Julian E. Barnes, Farnaz Fassihi and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad of Iran registering as a candidate in the presidential election in Tehran in 2024.

item.title: Iran Threatens to Strike Beyond the Middle East if the U.S. Resumes Attacks
item.description: The warning was issued as President Trump and Vice President JD Vance say progress is being made toward a deal, while keeping open the threat of renewed strikes.
item.dc:creator: Euan Ward
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: Members of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps stand near a drone display at a government-organized march in Tehran last month.

item.title: Senate Votes to Take Up Measure to Force Trump to End Iran War
item.description: With four Republican backers, Democrats won a vote to advance a resolution that would force the president to end hostilities or win authorization from Congress.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
item.media:description: The sliver of G.O.P. skepticism to the president’s handling of the Iran conflict widened last week.

item.title: How Iran Gained Leverage in the War
item.description: Outmatched militarily, Iran used “triangular coercion” by attacking Gulf states and closing the Strait of Hormuz. It points to a long-term U.S. vulnerability.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Taub
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A billboard in Enghelab Square in Tehran in April showing American military hardware trapped in a net.

item.title: Trump’s Spring Revenge Tour Routed G.O.P. Foes. But Fall Headwinds Loom.
item.description: He has used his sway with his base to oust wayward Republicans more than he has made inroads with the independents his party needs to defeat Democrats in November.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie, center, after his concession speech on Tuesday in Hebron, Ky. Mr. Massie, one of President Trump’s most outspoken critics in Congress, was ousted by a primary challenger.

item.title: Trump Crushes Republican Dissent: 8 Takeaways From Tuesday’s Primaries
item.description: President Trump unseated Representative Thomas Massie, a top Republican critic in Congress, and also got his way in other primary contests.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Michael Swensen for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Thomas Massie of Kentucky ran a robust campaign against his Trump-backed challenger but was defeated on Tuesday night.

item.title: Days After Hosting Trump, Xi Deepens Ties With Putin
item.description: China’s leader Xi Jinping called for a halt to fighting in the Middle East, ignored Russia’s war in Ukraine and took a veiled swipe at the United States.
item.dc:creator: David Pierson and Ivan Nechepurenko
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Maxim Shemetov
item.media:description: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia and the Chinese leader Xi Jinping inspecting an honor guard at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: China Confirms It Will Buy 200 Boeing Planes
item.description: Beijing’s acknowledgment on Wednesday that tariffs were discussed in talks with President Trump signals it was prepared to push back if the U.S. revives duties.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Catie Edmondson
item.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump attending a state banquet with China’s top leader, Xi Jinping, in Beijing last week.

item.title: Older People Are Still Underrepresented on TV, but Shows Like ‘The Boroughs’ Are Helping
item.description: While older people are still underrepresented on TV, the characters that are there have become richer and more multidimensional.
item.dc:creator: Alexis Soloski
item.category: A Man on the Inside (TV Program)
Only Murders in the Building (TV Program)

item.title: The San Diego Mosque Shootings Were a Crime Made for and by the Internet
item.description: Authorities said the teenagers who killed three men met online, posted their writings there and livestreamed the killings.
item.dc:creator: Tim Arango, Chelsia Rose Marcius, Madison Malone Kircher and Nicholas Bogel-Burroughs
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: A view of the Islamic Center of San Diego following a deadly mass shooting at the mosque on Monday.

item.title: Victims of San Diego Mosque Shooting Remembered as Heroes and Leaders
item.description: The three men killed in the shooting at the Islamic Center of San Diego were instrumental in delaying the attackers, the city’s police chief said.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales, Jill Cowan and Neil Vigdor
item.category: Islamic Center of San Diego Shooting (2026)
item.media:credit: John Francis Peters for The New York Times
item.media:description: Three men were shot dead on Monday at the Islamic Center of San Diego.

item.title: For the Obama Center, Mark Bradford Paints a Fierce and Luminous Chicago
item.description: “City of the Big Shoulders” reflects the city’s strength and complexity. It took the artist five years to complete, mapping migration patterns and structural racism.
item.dc:creator: Elly Fishman
item.media:credit: Evan Jenkins for The New York Times
item.media:description: The painter Mark Bradford stands at the edge of Lake Michigan on Chicago’s South Side. The artist drew inspiration from both the Great Lake and the city’s industrial roots for his new work commissioned by the Obama Foundation.

item.title: The French Civil Servant Accused of Drugging More Than 100 Women
item.description: For years, prosecutors have accused Christian Nègre, once a senior official, of slipping diuretics into women’s drinks. Despite admitting some accusations in an interview with a French daily, he is yet to stand trial.
item.dc:creator: Ségolène Le Stradic
item.media:credit: Juliette Pavy for The New York Times
item.media:description: A path along the River Ill in Strasbourg, France, where Marie-Hélène Brice said Christian Nègre brought her during a job interview.

item.title: How to Get a Pardon in Trump’s Washington
item.description: Fast-talking lawyers and lobbyists promise to get white-collar criminals out of jail — for a fee.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Toobin

item.title: Duffy Defends Road Trip Reality Show In Tense Senate Hearing
item.description: Democrats called the transportation secretary’s reality-show-like series unethical.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian and Chris Cameron
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy’s combative stance was a notable shift for someone who has presented himself as congenial and accessible.

item.title: Faulty Part in UPS Plane Crash Was Often Overlooked, Witnesses Say
item.description: Investigators suggested that infrequent maintenance requirements, among other issues, appeared to contribute to the faulty part that caused the crash, which killed 15 people last year.
item.dc:creator: Billy Witz and Karoun Demirjian
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry/Associated Press
item.media:description: Plumes of smoke near the area of a UPS cargo plane crash at Louisville Muhammad Ali International Airport in November.

item.title: Europe’s Green-Energy Future Has a Reindeer Problem
item.description: In northern Norway, Sami people fear a copper mine will disrupt their traditional lifestyles.
item.dc:creator: Alexa Robles-Gil, Sachi Kitajima Mulkey and Michal Siarek
item.media:credit: Michał Siarek for The New York Times

item.title: She Was Finding Sources of Dangerous Water and Soil Pollution
item.description: Melanie Malone led a research project to identify and study contamination sites in Washington State. Then the E.P.A. canceled her grant.
item.dc:creator: Sachi Kitajima Mulkey
item.media:credit: Meron Tekie Menghistab for The New York Times

item.title: ‘Whimsy,’ a New Trend, May Be a Life Raft for Gen-Z and Millennials
item.description: To a younger generation, the word describes a playful response to a difficult world — even if it might strike some as a precious form of capitalism.
item.dc:creator: Brian Josephs

item.title: Hatred of Israel and the Degradation of the West
item.description: Feeding narratives to the credulous isn’t speaking truth to power.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
Human Rights and Human Rights Violations
item.media:credit: Ronaldo Schemidt/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images

item.title: A Cattle Ranch Is Doing What Ivy League Colleges Can’t
item.description: College education does almost nothing to develop the sense that what we do in our day-to-day lives resonates with people beyond ourselves.
item.dc:creator: Michal Leibowitz

item.title: Who’s Writing This?
item.description: We look at the expanding role of A.I. in our lives.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.media:credit: Kelsey McClellan for The New York Times

item.title: Late Night Could Set a Clock by Trump’s Iran Attack Delays
item.description: Jimmy Kimmel noted that the president backs down on his own deadlines to bomb Iran “every Tuesday. It’s like a new episode of ‘NCIS.’”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)
The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)

item.title: Is the Bond Market Signaling Danger? Or Opportunity?
item.description: Interest rates for long-term Treasury bonds have surged to levels last seen in 2007, before the great financial crisis.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Sommer
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Josie Norton

item.title: The Growing Anxiety Over AI, Jobs and the Future
item.description: Layoffs linked to artificial intelligence are rattling workers and college graduates, stoking concerns worldwide that are showing up in polls.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Ross Sorkin, Bernhard Warner, Sarah Kessler, Michael J. de la Merced, Niko Gallogly and Brian O’Keefe
item.media:credit: Carlos Barria/Reuters
item.media:description: A.I.-related layoffs at Meta may be feeding into rising global anxiety about the consequences of companies embracing the technology.

item.title: Doctors Asked Officers to Unshackle a Patient. They Refused for 26 Days.
item.description: A lawsuit challenges the police practice of shackling mentally ill arrestees in New York, sometimes for long periods, while they await arraignment in locked psychiatric wards.
item.dc:creator: Andy Newman

item.title: Killing in Japan Stirs Fears of Tokuryu Crime Rings
item.description: Four teenage boys were arrested in the killing of a woman at her home. The police say they might have been directed by a new type of transient criminal network known as tokuryu.
item.dc:creator: Javier C. Hernández and Kiuko Notoya
item.media:credit: The Yomiuri Shimbun, Reuters
item.media:description: A view of the compound where a 69-year-old woman was killed in Kaminokawa, Japan, last week.
